map.note: The parser treats the inflated payload after the first 40 bytes as triangle vertices. This is suitable for visual debugging, not a full .map spec implementation.

grid.contains: Path grid definitions keyed by zone id.

grid_entries.contains: Path waypoints in native x/y/z coordinates.

doors.contains: Door/object marker coordinates and destination metadata.

zone_points.contains: Zone connection markers and target coordinates.

object.contains: Placed world objects with native coordinates.

ground_spawns.contains: Ground spawn regions; center point is derived for debug rendering.

conclusion: The local EQEmu .map file is the best local geometry source because it uses the same native coordinate family as spawn2.
Database grid/door/zone_point/object/ground_spawn/trap tables provide useful overlays and validation markers, not full visual map geometry.
The .nav file is likely pathing/navmesh data and is useful long-term, but was not parsed beyond file identification in this prototype.
This is better than PNG alignment because it avoids screenshot scaling/cropping/orientation problems.
caveats: The .map triangle parser is a conservative visual-debug parser, not a complete EQEmu map format implementation.
Cazic-Thule data appears to be the local PEQ/revamped-era zone content.
Z-level/floor separation still needs a later layer/filter pass.
